strengths: 20+ years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — exceeds the 15-year minimum requirement

Demonstrated entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, still active since 2016), shipped multiple open-source libraries, and built production systems used by NGOs globally
Active contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at current role, plus consistent public knowledge-sharing via conference talks (FOSS4G 2019) and multi-day technical workshops
concerns: AI expertise is superficial and recent — all AI-related work began April 2025; no ML/AI credentials, publications, or demonstrated AI products shipped at scale prior to this role
Cloud depth is absent — AWS CDK appears in a single pricing-team role at Optilyz; no cloud architecture, platform strategy, or infrastructure-at-scale experience is evidenced

Career level and scope fall well below director-level IC: titles peak at 'Staff Software Engineer'; no evidence of advising strategic customers, shaping cross-organizational AI strategy, or external recognition as an AI expert
response.justification: This role requires recognized AI expertise, director-level strategic influence, and deep cloud fluency — the candidate demonstrably lacks all three. AI exposure is confined to roughly 14 months of MCP tooling and agentic-workflow contributions at the current employer, with no AI products shipped before 2025, no relevant advanced credentials, and no external recognition in the AI field. Cloud experience amounts to a single-role use of AWS CDK for pricing logic, not platform-level or strategic cloud work. The highest IC title held is Staff Software Engineer, and the resume shows no evidence of advising enterprise customers, shaping organizational AI strategy, or inspiring engineering communities on AI at scale — the behaviors central to this role's responsibilities. The candidate's 20-year engineering career and entrepreneurial builder instinct (GISCollective, multiple open-source libraries) are genuine assets but cannot compensate for the core competency gaps required of a director-level AI strategy principal.
